description_long: This object is a stepped arrangement of rectangular stone blocks, forming a small pyramid-like structure with four distinct tiers. Each block has a smooth, slightly rounded edge and a uniform, light beige coloration, resembling natural sandstone or polished limestone. The blocks are precisely cut and closely fitted together, with no visible mortar or gaps between them. The surface texture appears matte and finely grained, suggesting a well-finished stone material. The overall form is geometric and symmetrical, with each tier decreasing in size as it ascends. This type of structure could serve as a decorative barrier, seating, or a low monument in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A stepped, pyramid-shaped arrangement of smooth, light beige stone blocks with a matte finish and precise, geometric form.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full stepped pyramid shape with four tiers, each block's face clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of each block and the staggered, layered arrangement of the tiers.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, displaying the same stepped structure and block faces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the block depth and the uniform stacking of the tiers.
attribute_location_context: urban plaza or landscaped park as a decorative barrier or seating element
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with no chips or discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: stacked in a stepped, interlocked arrangement
